title: poa versicolor subsp. varia
intro: Poa versicolor subsp. varia, also known as the blue grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is primarily found in the mountainous regions of Australia and is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in reducing fever and alleviating pain. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, with the leaves and roots being used for medicinal purposes.
